title: Test suite hangs on HeaderTests.test_headerFonts_areBold
description: The full xcodebuild test run wedges on 'FSNotesTests.HeaderTests.test_headerFonts_areBold' with no completion event. Observed during a clean run on 2026-04-29: 2657 cases scheduled, 1293 passed before the hang, 9 unrelated failures earlier in the run. The test entered 'started' state at ~10:17 and never produced 'passed' or 'failed' over the next 10+ minutes; xcodebuild process remains alive (state SN, sleeping) but writes no further output to either /tmp/xctest.log or the xcresult bundle. This blocks the suite from completing — every full run must be killed manually before any 'BUILD SUCCEEDED'/'TEST SUCCEEDED' conclusion is reached. Repro: xcodebuild test -workspace FSNotes.xcworkspace -scheme FSNotes -destination 'platform=macOS' > /tmp/xctest.log 2>&1. Wait ~1 minute past last 'Test Case' entry; if HeaderTests is the last started case with no terminal status, the hang has reproduced. Likely cause: the test depends on a window/font load that doesn't complete in the test environment — header font assertions probably need a live AppKit context that the bare xctest runner doesn't fully provide. Need to either gate this test on a window-context precondition, port the assertion to a pure-function test on Theme/DocumentRenderer, or skip it under non-interactive runs.

title: Phase5aDeleteBypassFixTests.test_insertCodeSpan_emptySelection_doesNotTripAssertion: code span markers not inserted
description:
Tests/Phase5aDeleteBypassFixTests.swift:206 fails:
  'XCTAssertEqual failed: ("Optional(\"hello\")") is not equal to ("Optional(\"hello\u0060\u0060\")")'

The test inserts an inline code span on an empty selection; expected resulting text is hello (two backticks appended), actual is hello (no markers). So the insertCodeSpan-with-empty-selection path is now a no-op that returns the original text rather than a code-span scaffold. Either (a) the empty-selection branch was changed to do nothing intentionally and the test wasn't updated, or (b) a bug in EditingOps.insertCodeSpan / toggleInlineTrait collapsed the empty-selection insertion. The test name implies its primary purpose is to verify the StorageWriteGuard debug assertion isn't tripped (a Phase 5a invariant); the assertion behavior may be correct now, but the markup-insertion side effect regressed.

Inspect EditingOps.insertCodeSpan / EditTextView formatting wiring; either fix the missing markers or update the test if the intentional behavior is no-op-on-empty-selection (and document why).

title: Phase1OllamaProviderTests.testOllamaStreamParser_handlesPartialLines: 'main flush' expectation times out after 1s
description:
Tests/Phase1OllamaProviderTests.swift:184 fails:
  'Asynchronous wait failed: Exceeded timeout of 1 seconds, with unfulfilled expectations: "main flush".'

The Ollama NDJSON stream parser test feeds partial lines and expects them to coalesce and flush on the main queue inside 1s. The flush expectation is never fulfilled. Likely related to the MCP wired-bridge timeout cluster (fsnotes-ptr) — same shape: an XCTestExpectation that depends on a callback marshaling back to the test thread that never arrives. Candidates:
  (a) Stream parser dispatches to a queue the test isn't draining (RunLoop.main vs DispatchQueue.main timing).
  (b) The 'partial line' branch never emits because the parser is gated on a newline that the test never feeds.
  (c) Recent changes to OllamaProvider.swift broke the partial-line accumulator.

Inspect OllamaProvider stream-parsing code path and the test's expectation-fulfillment site at line 184; cross-check against fsnotes-ptr.

title: paragraph + atStart + pressBackspace produces block-count delta -2 instead of -1
description:
Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift:501 fails:
  '[paragraph | atStart | pressBackspace | empty] block-count delta -2 does not match FSM row paragraph | atStart | pressBackspace → mergeWithPrevious expected window -1...-1. before=5 after=3.'

The FSM transition table declares pressing backspace at the start of a paragraph merges with the previous block (delta -1: one block disappears). The live edit instead produces a delta of -2 — two blocks disappear. Likely candidates:
  (a) A blankLine separator between the paragraphs is being consumed in addition to the merged paragraph.
  (b) The merge is taking the predecessor + a trailing blank, eating both.
  (c) EditingOps.deleteAtBoundary or mergeAdjacentBlocks composes two structural changes where the contract claims one.

Fix lives in EditingOps for the pressBackspace-at-start-of-paragraph case — the EditContract.declaredActions must match the actual count delta. Either correct the action sequence (e.g. add an explicit .deleteBlock for the swallowed blankLine) or change the merge primitive to leave the blankLine in place.

title: numberedList + pressTab does not round-trip through serialize→parse
description:
11 combinatorial scenarios in Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift:264 fail the strong invariant HTML(parse(serialize(doc))) == HTML(doc), all with the pattern [numberedList | <cursor> | pressTab | <selection>]:
  • numberedList × {atStart, midContent, atEnd, onEmptyBlock} × {empty, intraBlock, crossBlock}
  • Plus the corresponding pressTab-bearing sequences in test_combinatorialSequences: type.tab, tab.type, tab.tab, type.type.tab, type.tab.type, type.tab.ret, type.tab.bks, type.tab.tab.

Failure message: 'live Document does NOT round-trip through serialize→parse. Live HTML differs from re-parsed HTML — this state cannot survive save+reload.'

Implication: pressing Tab inside a numbered list produces a Document whose serialized markdown re-parses into a different Document. The user cannot save and reload without losing/changing structure. Likely cause: indent/outdent of an ordered-list item creates structure (e.g. nested ordered list with parent renumbered, or list-with-orphan continuation) that MarkdownSerializer emits in a form MarkdownParser then re-reads as a different shape — strong candidate is the marker/indent emission for nested ordered children. Per ARCHITECTURE.md → Outstanding bugs surfaced by the sweep, fixes here also need to remove the corresponding entries from Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt to prevent the now-passing scenarios from flipping to 'unexpectedly passed'.

title: BugInventoryRegressionTests bug6/bug8/bug25 fail: missing rows in FSMTransitions.swift
description:
Three tests in Tests/UserFlows/BugInventoryRegressionTests.swift fail with identical message 'Bug #N row is missing from FSMTransitions.swift':
  • Tests/UserFlows/BugInventoryRegressionTests.swift:139 → bug6 (returnAtStartOfHeading_createsEmptyParagraphBefore)
  • Tests/UserFlows/BugInventoryRegressionTests.swift:168 → bug8 (backspaceMergesParagraphs)
  • Tests/UserFlows/BugInventoryRegressionTests.swift:461 → bug25 (tab_onNumberedListL1_demotesToL2)

The harness expects fixture rows tagged with the corresponding bugId in Tests/Fixtures/FSMTransitions.swift; those rows are absent. Per ARCHITECTURE.md → Test alignment, bug rows with bugId != nil are wrapped in XCTExpectFailure(strict: true) so they fail-by-design until the FSM is fixed; the missing-row failure here is upstream of that — the regression-test scaffolding can't even find the row to wrap. Fix is to add three rows to FSMTransitions.swift covering the three transitions, with bugId set to 6, 8, and 25 respectively, then either let the strict-XCTExpectFailure path catch them as expected failures or implement the missing FSM behavior.

title: MCP wired-bridge tests time out with 'test never completed'
description: Five MCP tool tests fail with XCTAssertTrue(false) carrying message 'test never completed' after a ~5-second timeout: AppendToNoteToolTests.testHappyPathRoutesThroughWiredBridge (5.063s), ApplyFormattingToolTests.testHappyPathTogglesBoldThroughWiredBridge (5.788s), EditNoteToolTests.testHappyPathRoutesReplaceBlockThroughWiredBridge (5.071s), ExportPDFToolTests.testHappyPathExportsRealPDFThroughWiredBridge (5.690s), and one more in the same family (need to confirm full list once the suite completes — see related hang issue). All five share the 'testHappyPath*ThroughWiredBridge' naming pattern and the 'test never completed' assertion message at ~5s, indicating a shared XCTestExpectation timeout in the wired-bridge test fixture. Likely cause: AppBridge dispatch from the test thread never marshals back to the expectation's fulfill() — either (a) the bridge is gated on something only true at runtime (compositionSession state? main-thread assertion?), (b) MCPServer.handleToolCalls is dispatching to a queue the test isn't draining, or (c) the wired-bridge test harness was written against a previous AppBridge interface and silently skips the fulfill path. Files to inspect: Tests/MCP/AppendToNoteToolTests.swift:154, Tests/MCP/ApplyFormattingToolTests.swift:126, similar lines in the other three. AppBridgeImpl write path docs in ARCHITECTURE.md note 'refuses with BridgeEditOutcome.failed if compositionSession.isActive == true' — verify the test isn't tripping that gate.
notes: Confirmed 4 affected tests (not 5 as initially feared): AppendToNoteToolTests/testHappyPathRoutesThroughWiredBridge, ApplyFormattingToolTests/testHappyPathTogglesBoldThroughWiredBridge, EditNoteToolTests/testHappyPathRoutesReplaceBlockThroughWiredBridge, ExportPDFToolTests/testHappyPathExportsRealPDFThroughWiredBridge. Found via: grep -l 'testHappyPath.*ThroughWiredBridge' Tests/MCP/*.swift. The 11 other MCP tool test files (CreateNote, DeleteNote, GetCurrent/Folder/Projects/Tags, ListFolders/Notes, MoveNote, ReadNote, SearchNotes) do not use the wired-bridge fixture and are presumably not affected.

title: Bug #46: blockquote Return-then-Backspace mints fresh slot UUID (13 variants)
description:
Backspace after a Return-split inside a blockquote regenerates the projection slot UUID instead of mutating in place. Harness contract failure (~block@2 slot identity not preserved). 13 sequence variants, single root cause. Same shape as Bug #38 (already fixed for lists) but for blockquote split-and-merge.

Failing scenarios (length-2 + length-3 sequence matrix):
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks
- seq | blockquote | type.ret.bks
- seq | blockquote | ret.ret.bks
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks.type
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks.ret
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks.bks
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks.tab
- seq | blockquote | ret.bks.stab
- seq | blockquote | ret.tab.bks
- seq | blockquote | ret.stab.bks
- seq | blockquote | bks.ret.bks
- seq | blockquote | tab.ret.bks
- seq | blockquote | stab.ret.bks

Layer: combinatorial sequence sweep
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialSequences

Reference fix: Bug #38 was the same root cause for lists — EditingOps.deleteInList collapses an only-item removal to .paragraph(inline: []) keeping the same slot. The fix here likely involves analogous slot-preservation in the blockquote split/merge path.
acceptance_criteria: All 13 sequence scenarios flip to red in test_combinatorialSequences; 13 lines removed from DiscoveredBugs.txt; ~block@2 slot identity preserved across the Return-then-Backspace sequence; round-trip parity holds.

title: Bug #44: numbered-list type.ret.tab sequence doesn't round-trip
description:
Real user flow (extend list with sub-item) produces state that won't survive serialize→parse. Length-3 sequence on a numbered list seed.

Failing scenario:
- seq | numberedList | type.ret.tab

Layer: combinatorial sequence sweep (composability matrix)
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialSequences
acceptance_criteria: Scenario flips to red in test_combinatorialSequences; line removed from DiscoveredBugs.txt; round-trip parity holds.

title: Bug #43: display-math block edits don't round-trip (9 variants)
description:
Display-math source presence/absence in the serialized form doesn't match the live projection — likely the rendered fragment owns the display-math source while the projection holds an empty inline. 9 variants, single root cause.

Failing scenarios:
- displayMathBlock | atStart | pressReturn | intraBlock
- displayMathBlock | atStart | pressBackspace | intraBlock
- displayMathBlock | atStart | pressForwardDelete | empty
- displayMathBlock | atStart | pressForwardDelete | intraBlock
- displayMathBlock | atEnd | pressReturn | intraBlock
- displayMathBlock | atEnd | pressBackspace | empty
- displayMathBlock | atEnd | pressBackspace | intraBlock
- displayMathBlock | atEnd | pressForwardDelete | empty
- displayMathBlock | atEnd | pressForwardDelete | intraBlock

Layer: combinatorial single-action sweep
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialCoverage
acceptance_criteria: All 9 scenarios flip to red; 9 lines removed from DiscoveredBugs.txt; round-trip parity holds for all 9.

title: Bug #42: HR backspace at atStart doesn't round-trip
description:
Backspace at the boundary before an HR should remove it (or merge with the predecessor) but produces state that won't survive serialize→parse.

Failing scenario:
- horizontalRule | atStart | pressBackspace | empty

Layer: combinatorial single-action sweep
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialCoverage
acceptance_criteria: Scenario flips to red; line removed from DiscoveredBugs.txt; round-trip parity holds.

title: Bug #41: cross-block selection through a table corrupts state
description:
Cross-block selection that includes a table boundary corrupts the table block on Return / Backspace / ForwardDelete. 3 sequence variants, single root cause.

Failing scenarios (combinatorial single-action matrix):
- table | atEnd | pressReturn | crossBlock
- table | atEnd | pressBackspace | crossBlock
- table | atEnd | pressForwardDelete | crossBlock

Layer: combinatorial single-action sweep
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialCoverage
acceptance_criteria: All 3 scenarios flip to red in CombinatorialCoverageTests; the 3 lines are removed from Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt in the same commit; round-trip parity holds for all 3 scenarios.

title: Bug #40: blockquote forward-delete at atEnd doesn't round-trip
description:
Forward-delete at the end of a blockquote line merges with the next block, but produces state that won't survive save+reload. Same shape as Bug #39 but inside a blockquote — likely shares the merge-into-blockquote-trailing-newline root cause.

Failing scenario (combinatorial single-action matrix):
- blockquote | atEnd | pressForwardDelete | empty

Layer: combinatorial single-action sweep
Test: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialCoverage
Tracking: wrapped in XCTExpectFailure(strict: true) via Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt.
acceptance_criteria: Scenario flips to red ('unexpectedly passed') in CombinatorialCoverageTests; the corresponding line is removed from Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt in the same commit; the round-trip invariant HTML(parse(serialize(doc))) == HTML(doc) holds for the scenario.

title: Bug #39: paragraph forward-delete at atEnd doesn't round-trip
description:
Forward-delete at the end of a paragraph merges with the next block, but produces state that won't survive save+reload (HTML(parse(serialize(doc))) != HTML(doc)).

**Failing scenario** (combinatorial single-action matrix):
`paragraph | atEnd | pressForwardDelete | empty`

**Layer**: combinatorial single-action sweep
**Test**: Tests/Combinatorial/CombinatorialCoverageTests.swift::test_combinatorialCoverage
**Tracking**: wrapped in XCTExpectFailure(strict: true) via Tests/Combinatorial/DiscoveredBugs.txt — entry must be deleted when fixed.

**Acceptance**: scenario flips to red ("unexpectedly passed") in CombinatorialCoverageTests, DiscoveredBugs.txt line removed in same commit.
